project.title: The small but versatile DSS1 protein is required for proper Integrator-PP2A function
project.projectDescription: This study highlight the role of DSS1 as an integral component of the INTAC backbone module, that is important for maintaining the stability of INTAC to support its diverse functions in transcription regulation.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Cells were lysed using a buffer containing 1% SDC, 10 mM tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine (TCEP), 20 mM chloroacetamide (CAA), and 1× protease inhibitor cocktail. The lysate was heated at 95 °C for 60 minutes, followed by ultrasonication. Protein concentration was determined using the Pierce BCA Protein Assay Kit. For protein digestion, trypsin was added at a 1:50 (enzyme-to-substrate, w/w) ratio, and the mixture was incubated overnight at 37 °C. After digestion, trifluoroacetic acid (TFA) was added to terminate the reaction. The sample was then centrifuged, and the supernatant was desalted using a C18 column. Finally, the peptides were lyophilized to dryness and stored at −20 °C for further analysis.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Data were analyzed using PEAKS Online (version 12 build 2.1.2024-11-20_160513). The following parameters were applied: MS1 mass tolerance was set to 10 ppm, and MS2 mass tolerance was set to 0.02 Da. The UniProt human protein database (20,375 entries) was used for database searching. Carbamidomethylation of cysteine was specified as a fixed modification, while oxidation of methionine and N-terminal acetylation were considered variable modifications. A false discovery rate (FDR) of 1% was used as the cutoff for peptide, protein, and peptide-spectrum match (PSM) identification.
